title: The Industrial Revolution
puzzle: Why did machines change daily life more deeply than many rulers ever did?
insight: The Industrial Revolution shifted production from hand labor to machine-powered systems, transforming work, cities, transport, and the scale of manufacturing.
key_ideas: Steam power accelerated production and movement.
Industrialization changed social life, not only economics.
mind_blower: A new machine can quietly reorganize an entire society without ever making a speech.
next_curiosity: If industrialization transformed work then, what technology is reshaping work now?
narration_text: Why did machines change daily life more deeply than many rulers ever did? The Industrial Revolution shifted production from hand labor to machine-powered systems, transforming work, cities, transport, and the scale of manufacturing. Factories concentrated labor and machinery. Steam power accelerated production and movement. Industrialization changed social life, not only economics. A new machine can quietly reorganize an entire society without ever making a speech. If industrialization transformed work then, what technology is reshaping work now?
